identity.fantasy: A compact forest courier who keeps opponents honest with a short-range compressed-air pistol.
silhouette: Teal hood framing two rounded ears
Large ringed tail and rust-orange side satchel
Short brass air-pistol held close to the body

primary.name: Breeze Pop
primary.counterplay: Advance during the visible crouched wind-up, bait the shot, then punish Nori during the open recovery pose.

ability.name: Tailwind Brake
ability.description: Nori plants her feet and releases a short rearward air burst that creates space but leaves her stationary during the wind-up.
ability.counterplay: Jump over the low burst or wait outside its short range; Nori cannot move while priming it.
ability.interruption: If interrupted before the spawn frame, the cooldown is not spent and no burst is created.

weaknesses: The pistol has short range and loses to opponents who can stay just outside its effective push distance.
Both the shot recovery and ability wind-up expose Nori to a close-range punish.

artBrief.spriteSheet: Each body action is an independent transparent sheet. Idle is a 2x2 sheet; walk is a 2x3 sheet read left-to-right then top-to-bottom; attack is a 3x3 sheet; hurt is a 2x2 sheet. Use feet pivot (0.5, 0.9). Keep the air-pistol, muzzle puff, projectile, and impact as separate renderer layers.
artBrief.windUpCue: Nori lowers her stance, brings both hands tight around the brass pistol, and visibly braces before the hit frame.
artBrief.impactCue: At attack frame 4, spawn a separate compact air-puff and play a single simulation-authoritative hit event; the body sheet contains only recoil.
artBrief.recoveryCue: Nori opens her stance and lowers the pistol for two readable frames after firing.
avoid: Named franchise characters, assets, logos, and terminology
Detached muzzle flashes, projectiles, impacts, dust, or motion lines inside body sheets
Unreadable detail or frame-to-frame silhouette drift at mobile scale

implementationNotes.notes: The simulation/server emits and resolves hit, spawn, knockback, death, and state transitions. The renderer only plays frames and displays event-driven VFX. Damage is zero in this ring-out prototype; primary knockback must be applied only at attack event frame 4.
